source: All-In Podcast — Markets: pricing in tariffs, Trump vs Powell, the $10 trillion AGI prize
summary: Being the lowest-cost token producer will be the defining competitive advantage in AI — a structural shift from prior tech cycles

tickers_audited.reasoning: Baker did not specifically name NVDA in connection with the lowest-cost token producer thesis. NVDA is infrastructure that others use; the thesis is about who wins by being the lowest-cost producer, not about the hardware supplier. Baker's structural argument favors companies with proprietary silicon over those relying on expensive third-party compute — which actually positions NVDA as something to be displaced, not a direct beneficiary. No specific connection made by Baker between NVDA and this thesis.

While GOOGL structurally fits the thesis (TPUs, scale, vertical integration), Baker did not specifically name Google as a beneficiary of the lowest-cost token producer thesis in this interview. The thesis summary explicitly states 'Baker does not name specific winners explicitly.' Applying the strict test: no specific verbal connection made.

Same issue as GOOGL — META structurally fits the thesis but Baker did not specifically name Meta as a beneficiary of the lowest-cost token producer thesis. The thesis summary confirms Baker named no specific winners. No specific verbal connection made.
